attractions.name: Tracy's King Crab Shack
attractions.season: Cruise season only, roughly early May to late September; closed winter
attractions.blurb: Open-air shack on the Juneau cruise dock: Bering Sea king crab legs, crab bisque, and crab-leg buckets.
attractions.tip: Lines peak 11am-2pm when four ships are in; go before 10:30 or after 5pm. Outdoor seating only.
attractions.duration_note: 1hr stop on a Juneau port day

attractions.name: Gold Creek Salmon Bake
attractions.season: Mid-May to late September, nightly seatings
attractions.blurb: All-you-can-eat alder-grilled salmon in a creekside forest amphitheater with gold panning and old mine ruins.
attractions.tip: Price includes the downtown shuttle; last seating around 8:30pm. Covered outdoor seating - bring a jacket.

attractions.name: Alaskan Brewing Company
attractions.season: Tasting room year-round; longest hours in summer
attractions.blurb: Alaska's oldest operating brewery (1986), home of Alaskan Amber; tasting room and short tour in Lemon Creek.
attractions.tip: Five miles from downtown in an industrial park - take the brewery shuttle or a cab. Pours are small samples.

attractions.name: Silver Gulch Brewing & Bottling
attractions.season: Year-round restaurant and taproom
attractions.blurb: America's northernmost brewery, 10mi up the Steese at Fox: long beer list, burgers, halibut, roadhouse room.
attractions.tip: Standard dinner stop coming back from Chena Hot Springs or a gold-dredge tour; kitchen closes around 9pm.
attractions.duration_note: dinner stop en route to Chena

attractions.name: HooDoo Brewing Co.
attractions.season: Year-round taproom, Tue-Sat; food trucks outside in summer
attractions.blurb: German-style Fairbanks brewery with a gravel beer garden - the local default pint after a Denali or Dalton drive.
attractions.tip: No kitchen; food trucks park outside. Free brewery tour Saturdays at 4pm, and it's closed Sun-Mon.

attractions.name: Midnight Sun Brewing Co.
attractions.season: Year-round; upstairs kitchen closes earlier than the taproom
attractions.blurb: Anchorage craft brewery behind Sockeye Red IPA and Berserker stout, with an upstairs kitchen in an industrial park.
attractions.tip: Off Old Seward at Dimond Hook - not walkable from downtown. The Loft kitchen shuts well before the taproom.

attractions.name: 49th State Brewing - Anchorage
attractions.season: Year-round; rooftop deck usable roughly May-September
attractions.blurb: Downtown brewpub with a rooftop deck over Cook Inlet toward Mt. Susitna; big menu, house beer, live music.
attractions.tip: Rooftop is first-come and fills by 6pm on clear evenings; three blocks from the Alaska Railroad depot.

attractions.name: Denali Brewing Company Tasting Room
attractions.season: Downtown tasting room mid-May to September; Twister Creek restaurant year-round
attractions.blurb: Talkeetna's brewery: a downtown tasting room plus a beer garden and full restaurant out on the Spur Road.
attractions.tip: Growler fills come from the downtown tasting room; the Spur Road beer garden has the lawn games and food.

attractions.name: Talkeetna Roadhouse
attractions.season: Year-round; breakfast 7am until the kitchen sells out
attractions.blurb: 1917 log roadhouse: sourdough hotcakes from a century-old starter, plate-size cinnamon rolls, bunks upstairs.
attractions.tip: Breakfast is family-style at shared tables, 7-11am. Order a half cinnamon roll - a full one feeds three.

attractions.name: Moose's Tooth Pub & Pizzeria
attractions.season: Year-round
attractions.blurb: Anchorage institution pouring its own beer beside huge specialty pizzas; among the busiest independent pizzerias in the US.
attractions.tip: Fri/Sat waits run 60-90min - join the online waitlist first, or take out and skip the room entirely.

attractions.name: Snow City Cafe
attractions.season: Year-round, breakfast and lunch daily from 7am
attractions.blurb: Downtown Anchorage's default breakfast: crab cake Benedict, reindeer sausage hash, open 7am every day.
attractions.tip: Weekend waits pass 45min - use the online waitlist from your hotel, or walk in between 7 and 8am.

attractions.name: Fat Ptarmigan
attractions.season: Year-round; closed Sundays
attractions.blurb: Small downtown wood-fired pizza room with an all-Alaska draft list, one block from the Anchorage Museum.
attractions.tip: Closed Sundays, no reservations - grab the bar if tables are full. Good rainy-day pairing with the museum.

attractions.name: M.A.'s Gourmet Dogs (reindeer dogs)
attractions.season: Cart runs roughly May-September, lunch hours, weather permitting
attractions.blurb: Downtown street cart at 4th & E doing the Anchorage reindeer sausage dog with grilled onions - the classic cheap bite.
attractions.tip: Lunch only and gone by mid-afternoon or in hard rain; the line looks long but moves in a few minutes.

attractions.name: Two Sisters Bakery
attractions.season: Year-round; longest hours in summer
attractions.blurb: Old Town Homer bakery in a converted house: morning buns, savory galettes, coffee, deck above Bishop's Beach.
attractions.tip: Pastries sell out by mid-morning in July. It's in Old Town, not on the Spit - about 4mi from the harbor.

attractions.name: Ray's Waterfront
attractions.season: Seasonal, roughly mid-May to September
attractions.blurb: Harborside Seward seafood room - grilled halibut, salmon, chowder - with trophy fish on the wall and boats out the window.
attractions.tip: Book the 5pm seating or expect an hour wait once the day cruises dock around 5:30-6pm.

attractions.name: Skagway Brewing Company
attractions.season: Year-round; busiest when cruise ships are in port
attractions.blurb: Skagway brewpub known for its spruce-tip ale, with a full kitchen two blocks off Broadway from the cruise docks.
attractions.tip: Spruce Tip Blonde is the one to order; the room empties and gets pleasant after the last ship sails.
attractions.duration_note: lunch on a Skagway port day

attractions.name: Salty Dawg Saloon
attractions.season: Open year-round, but the Spit is a summer scene
attractions.blurb: Lighthouse-topped log bar on Homer Spit, sawdust floor, ceiling and walls shingled with signed dollar bills.
attractions.tip: Bring a dollar and a marker to staple your own bill up. Opens late morning and is packed by 5pm in July.

attractions.name: Red Onion Saloon
attractions.season: May to September, with the cruise season
attractions.blurb: 1898 gold-rush saloon on Broadway with the original upstairs brothel; costumed madams run 15-min tours of the cribs.
attractions.tip: Tours sell out on four-ship days - sign up when you arrive, then drink downstairs until your slot comes up.

attractions.name: Fairview Inn
attractions.season: Year-round; live music most summer nights
attractions.blurb: 1923 Talkeetna bar where President Harding stopped weeks before he died; low ceilings, live bands, climbers' hangout.
attractions.tip: Music starts around 9pm and the room is tiny - the back beer garden is the overflow. Rooms upstairs are basic.

attractions.name: Chilkoot Charlie's
attractions.season: Year-round, nights only
attractions.blurb: Sprawling Spenard nightclub: sawdust floors, multiple stages and a dozen themed bars under one tin roof.
attractions.tip: Opens late (8pm-ish) and charges cover on band nights; take a cab - it is not a walk from downtown hotels.

attractions.name: Board of Trade Saloon
attractions.season: Year-round; wildest during Iditarod finish week in March
attractions.blurb: Front Street dive billed as the oldest saloon on the Bering Sea, where mushers and beach gold miners drink.
attractions.tip: Peak scene is Iditarod week in March. Nome is fly-in only, so price the airfare before planning the beer.

attractions.name: Brown Bear Saloon
attractions.season: Year-round roadside bar on the Seward Highway
attractions.blurb: Log roadhouse bar at Indian on Turnagain Arm, the surviving stop after the legendary Bird House Bar burned in 1996.
attractions.tip: Mile ~103 Seward Hwy, 25min from Anchorage; the Bird House site down the road is gone, so this is the stand-in.
attractions.duration_note: quick stop on the Seward Hwy

attractions.name: Kennicott Glacier Lodge
attractions.season: Late May to mid-September only
attractions.blurb: Restored lodge on the Kennecott mill-town boardwalk with the Root Glacier off the porch; family-style meals.
attractions.tip: Park at the McCarthy footbridge and shuttle in - no private cars past it. Book by winter for July dates.

attractions.name: Brooks Lodge
attractions.season: Open June 1 to mid-September; July for peak bear numbers
attractions.blurb: The only lodge at Brooks Camp: 16 cabins a short boardwalk from the Brooks Falls bear-viewing platforms.
attractions.tip: Rooms go by lottery about 18 months out (entry opens Dec 1); otherwise day-trip in by floatplane from Homer.
attractions.duration_note: 1-2 nights; mandatory bear school on arrival

attractions.name: Camp Denali & North Face Lodge
attractions.season: Early June to early September
attractions.cost: $3,000+ per person for a 3-night stay, all-inclusive
attractions.blurb: Deep-park lodges near Mile 89 with a Denali view from every cabin porch; guided hikes, no wifi, communal meals.
attractions.tip: Park Road is still severed at Mile 43 by the Pretty Rocks slide, so access is by charter flight to Kantishna.

attractions.name: Sheldon Chalet
attractions.season: Open year-round; Feb-Mar for aurora, Apr-Aug for climbing-season light
attractions.blurb: Five-room chalet on a granite nunatak in the Don Sheldon Amphitheater of the Ruth Glacier, ringed by mile-high walls.
attractions.tip: Helicopter in from Talkeetna; weather routinely strands guests a day, so leave slack before any flight home.

attractions.name: Winterlake Lodge
attractions.season: Summer June-September; winter Feb-March for dogsledding and aurora
attractions.blurb: Iditarod Trail lodge on Finger Lake, 45min by floatplane from Anchorage: cooking school, sled kennel, alpine hikes.
attractions.tip: It is a real Iditarod checkpoint - the race passes the door in early March. Floatplane summer, ski plane winter.

attractions.name: Tordrillo Mountain Lodge
attractions.season: Heli-skiing March-May; fishing and adventure packages June-September
attractions.blurb: Judd Lake heli-ski and adventure lodge in the Tordrillo Mountains, 40min by floatplane west of Anchorage.
attractions.tip: Spring corn-snow heli weeks sell out a year ahead; summer swaps skis for glacier trekking and king salmon.

attractions.name: Ultima Thule Lodge
attractions.season: Roughly mid-February to March and May to September
attractions.blurb: Family-run lodge on the Chitina River deep inside Wrangell-St. Elias, 100mi from the nearest road; daily bush flights.
attractions.tip: The flying is the product - a different gravel bar or glacier each day, and weather dictates the schedule.

attractions.name: Tutka Bay Lodge
attractions.season: Mid-May to mid-September
attractions.blurb: Cove lodge in Kachemak Bay 30min by boat from Homer, with a cooking school on a converted Bering Sea crab boat.
attractions.tip: Two-night minimum, and the water taxi runs on tides, so arrival and departure times shift every day.

attractions.name: Waterfall Resort
attractions.region: Southeast Alaska - Prince of Wales Island
attractions.season: Late May to early September fishing season
attractions.blurb: A 1912 salmon cannery on Prince of Wales Island turned all-inclusive fishing lodge with guided boats for king and coho.
attractions.tip: Floatplane in from Ketchikan; packages cover gear, guide and freezing/shipping your fish home.

attractions.name: Santa Claus House
attractions.season: Open year-round; reindeer pen outside, December is peak
attractions.blurb: Year-round Christmas store in North Pole with a 42ft Santa, a live reindeer pen and streets named Santa Claus Lane.
attractions.tip: You can order a letter to a kid postmarked North Pole in December; it's 20min south of Fairbanks off the Richardson.

attractions.name: Igloo City
attractions.season: Roadside exterior view year-round; the building never opened and is derelict
attractions.blurb: Four-story concrete igloo shell beside the Parks Highway - a 1970s hotel that never opened and now rots in place.
attractions.tip: Photo stop from the gravel pullout only; the structure is fenced, unsafe and posted against trespassing.

attractions.name: Mukluk Land
attractions.season: Open summer evenings only, roughly June through August
attractions.blurb: Junkyard theme park west of Tok: giant mukluk sign, mini-golf, a Santa rocket and sheds of hoarded Alaskana.
attractions.tip: Short summer-evening hours and cash-preferred; it's the classic why-not stop when driving in from Canada.

attractions.name: Begich Towers, Whittier
attractions.season: Year-round, but tunnel hours limit access
attractions.cost: Free to look; $13 tunnel toll per car
attractions.blurb: A 14-story former Army barracks where most of Whittier's ~270 residents live, with school, store, clinic and PO inside.
attractions.tip: It is private housing - look from the street only. The tunnel runs one-way on a published half-hour schedule.

attractions.name: Hammer Museum
attractions.season: Roughly mid-May to mid-September, Mon-Sat
attractions.blurb: One-room Main Street museum in Haines holding 2,000+ hammers, from Roman nail-pullers to whaling tools.
attractions.tip: Volunteer-run with short hours - check before walking up, then pair it with the Sheldon Museum two blocks away.

attractions.name: Musk Ox Farm
attractions.season: Daily tours mid-May to September; limited winter tours
attractions.blurb: Nonprofit Palmer farm raising domesticated muskoxen for qiviut wool, with 45-min guided tours right up to the fence.
attractions.tip: Calves appear in late spring; May-June is combing season, when you can watch qiviut come off the animals.

attractions.name: Reindeer Farm
attractions.season: Daily mid-May to early September; reduced hours off-season
attractions.blurb: Old Matanuska Colony farm under Pioneer Peak where you walk into the pen and hand-feed reindeer; also elk and bison.
attractions.tip: Wear boots - the pens are muddy. Combines with the Musk Ox Farm and Hatcher Pass into one Palmer day.

attractions.name: Bus 142 movie replica at 49th State Brewing
attractions.aka: Magic Bus, Into the Wild bus prop
attractions.season: Brewpub mid-May to mid-September; the bus sits in the lot and is visible anytime
attractions.blurb: The Fairbanks city bus prop from the film Into the Wild, parked outside 49th State Brewing in Healy for photos.
attractions.tip: The real Bus 142 was airlifted off the Stampede Trail in 2020 and now sits at the UA Museum of the North.

attractions.name: Alaska State Fair giant cabbage weigh-off
attractions.season: 12 days ending Labor Day, late August to early September
attractions.blurb: Palmer's late-August fair, home of the giant-vegetable weigh-off: 100lb cabbages, 2,000lb pumpkins, rodeo, midway.
attractions.tip: The cabbage weigh-off happens the opening weekend - go then if the giant vegetables are why you're coming.

attractions.name: Bunker Hill WWII bunkers
attractions.season: Snow-free and walkable roughly June to September
attractions.blurb: Concrete WWII command bunkers and gun positions on the hill above Dutch Harbor, with the whole port laid out below.
attractions.tip: Steep gravel road up; a rental truck or a local tour gets you there. Fort Schwatka on Ulakta Head is the bigger site.

attractions.name: Reflection Pond
attractions.season: June to early September; no road access while the Park Road is closed at Mile 43
attractions.blurb: The classic Denali-mirrored-in-water shot: a small kettle pond near Wonder Lake at Mile 85 of the Park Road.
attractions.tip: Pretty Rocks landslide still cuts the road at Mile 43, so reaching it means flying to Kantishna or a lodge stay.
attractions.duration_note: part of a multi-day deep-park trip

attractions.name: Denali View South
attractions.aka: Denali South Viewpoint, Parks Hwy Mile 135
attractions.season: Highway open year-round; clearest views May-September
attractions.blurb: Parks Highway wayside in Denali State Park with a boardwalk deck and panels aimed straight at Denali, 40mi off.
attractions.tip: Odds of a clear peak are best early morning. If Denali is out here, photograph it now rather than gambling north.
